title: Clompanus cuneata

The species is native to Australia and is often found in the eastern and southern regions. Clompanus cuneata is a shrub or small tree, typically growing up to 2-4 meters in height. Further classification may include its order and kingdom, but this information is not readily available.
uses: Clompanus cuneata has been traditionally used in various medicinal applications. Its roots are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat fever and rheumatism. In traditional medicine, the plant's leaves are used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant's roots are also used to treat digestive problems and are believed to have antibacterial properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Clompanus cuneata for medicinal purposes.
